commit.message:
feat(scaffolder): add AzureGroupPicker custom field extension with TDD tests

Replaces the free-text aks_admin_group_object_id UUID input with a dropdown
listing all Azure AD groups the logged-in user is a member of (including
nested/transitive memberships).

Uses the existing Microsoft SSO session (microsoftAuthApiRef) to obtain a
Microsoft Graph token via the GroupMember.Read.All delegated scope.
The browser calls the Graph API directly (CORS supported — no backend proxy).

Display format: 'Group Name (object-id)'
Emitted value:  Group Object ID UUID only

Fallback: if the Graph fetch fails, degrades to a plain text input
with UUID pattern validation.

New files:
  packages/app/src/scaffolder/AzureGroupPicker.tsx
  packages/app/src/scaffolder/AzureGroupPicker.test.tsx  (13 tests, TDD-first)

Modified:
  packages/app/src/scaffolder/index.ts  (add AzureGroupPicker to scaffolderModule)
  packages/app/src/scaffolder/AzureSubscriptionPicker.test.tsx  (fix sessionState$ mock type)

Prerequisite (one-time, done by an admin):
  Azure Portal > App registrations > Backstage app > API permissions
  Add: Microsoft Graph > GroupMember.Read.All (Delegated)
  Grant admin consent.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Improve MIDI transport read performance based on BTS analysis

- Add BTS byte-rate pacing (msgBytes*1000/3125 + 20ms DeviceInterval)
  before every send/request in KatanaSession to prevent device flooding
- Raise DefaultRequestTimeout from 1.5s to 15s to match BTS ProductSetting.timeout
- Widen MaximumSpanLength from 8 to 32 bytes (BTS uses SYSEX_MAXLEN=128);
  this batches ChainPattern + variation colors (0x39-3D) into 1 read
  instead of 4 separate requests
- Reduce ReadSyncInterval from 4s to 2s (phases are faster with less I/O)
- Extract ApplyDeviceState() helper to centralize suppressChangeTracking
  guard, replacing 9 scattered try/finally blocks
- Add exponential backoff on write flush failures:
  min(100 * 2^retryCount, 2000)ms before re-arming the write timer

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update daily-report workflow: 9 AM schedule, actions:read permission, improved instructions (#2)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(logging): add OTEL disable env var overrides (issue #400) (#401)

* fix(logging): add OTEL disable env var overrides (issue #400)

Add OTEL_SDK_DISABLED, OTEL_LOGS_DISABLED, OTEL_TRACES_DISABLED, and
OTEL_METRICS_DISABLED env var overrides to applyDefaults in
libs/go/logging. These take priority over Config field values, allowing
operators to disable OTEL signals at runtime without code changes.

OTEL_SDK_DISABLED follows the OpenTelemetry spec and disables all three
signals. When the OTLP collector is unavailable (e.g. bare-metal hosts),
setting OTEL_SDK_DISABLED=true prevents the exporter from dialling
127.0.0.1:4317, eliminating the log buffer saturation and cascading
RabbitMQ heartbeat loss described in the bug.

Closes #400

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/whale-net/everything/sessions/460645f9-c02a-4bd4-9943-123d1e9318dc

commit.message:
Rename kubelicate to kuberic

Rename all occurrences of kubelicate/Kubelicate/KUBELICATE to
kuberic/Kuberic/KUBERIC across the entire repository:
- Crate directories: kubelicate-core → kuberic-core, etc.
- Rust identifiers: KubelicateError → KubericError, KubelicateSet → KubericSet
- Proto file: kubelicate.proto → kuberic.proto
- K8s manifests: kubelicateset.yaml → kubericset.yaml
- All documentation references
- Environment variables: KUBELICATE_* → KUBERIC_*

86 files changed. Zero clippy warnings, all 46 tests pass.

repository.name: orion-ui
repository.description: Orion is a retro-styled, cross-platform UI framework written in C. The framework is not embedded or mobile-targeted — it is a desktop GUI framework designed for Linux, macOS, and Windows. Its central design philosophy is to faithfully reproduce the Windows API (Win32) message-based programming model in a portable, hardware-accelerated context.

commit.message:
fix: run all Dashboard API calls in single allSettled batch with 5s timeout

Previous approach had 3 sequential batches x 10s timeout = 30s total,
which matched the test timeout exactly. Now all 8 API calls run in a
single Promise.allSettled batch with 5s timeouts, guaranteeing loadAll
completes within ~5s even if all calls hang.

commit.message:
fix: poll Ynison state during pause instead of blocking on event

Same-track resume doesn't trigger _track_changed_event (only track
change and seek do). Poll every 1s with a 30s deadline so the stream
unblocks immediately when Ynison reports is_paused=False.

commit.message:
fix: upgrade auth failure logging from debug to warn

JWT validation failures and auth rejections were only logged at debug level,
making it impossible to see WHY Bearer tokens were rejected in production logs.

Changes:
- JWT validation failure: debug → warn with error message
- Userinfo fallback failure: debug → warn with error message
- Auth middleware 401: now logs route, method, and whether a Bearer token was present

This helps diagnose GT3 app 401 errors (expired tokens during background reconnect).

commit.message:
fix: address all 8 issues from multi-model code review

- Fix full-balance sell detection by checking preTokenBalances for
  tokens absent from postTokenBalances (ATA closed during swap)
- Add pagination loop in pollWallet to avoid dropping transactions
  when activity exceeds the per-poll limit
- Guard against null getTransaction responses to prevent TypeError
- Seed cursor on first poll without processing historical transactions
  to avoid flooding alerts for newly watched wallets
- Add drain() method to WhaleTracker so shutdown waits for in-flight
  polls before closing the database
- Log warning when whale alerts cannot be forwarded to Telegram due
  to missing TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID
- Add address length validation to CLI /watch command (consistent with
  Telegram bot and agent tool validation)
- Remove misleading --legacy reference from /configure help text

commit.message:
Add clipboard copy/paste support (l_clipboard_set/get)

Windows: Uses Win32 OpenClipboard/SetClipboardData/GetClipboardData (CF_TEXT).
X11: Full selection protocol — InternAtom for CLIPBOARD/UTF8_STRING/TARGETS,
  SetSelectionOwner to claim, SelectionRequest/SelectionNotify event handling
  for cross-app paste, ConvertSelection+GetProperty for paste-from-others.
Framebuffer/WASI: Internal static buffer (within-process clipboard).

New API in l_gfx.h:
  l_clipboard_set(canvas, text, len) — copy text to clipboard
  l_clipboard_get(canvas, buf, max) — paste from clipboard

Tests: test_clipboard.c (10 assertions — roundtrip, overwrite, truncation)
README: Updated header table and function reference

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix map canvas clipping: remove explicit height, use min()+aspect-ratio

Having both width:420px and height:420px meant when max-width:100%
shrank the width, height stayed 420px — making the canvas taller than
its container so the top got clipped by overflow:hidden.

Now: width:min(420px,100%) lets it fill the panel up to 420px,
and aspect-ratio:1/1 derives height from width — always square,
never overflowing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add crash types 19-26, fix post-build analysis accuracy

Crash types added:
- Type 19: RESOURCE_SHRUNK_BY_NAME — getIdentifier() with strict shrinkResources
- Type 20: GENERIC_SIGNATURE_STRIPPED — TypeToken generic type erasure
- Type 21: COMPONENT_CLASS_NOT_FOUND — ComponentName/setClassName string reference
- Type 22: DATA_CLASS_MEMBER_STRIPPED — copy()/componentN() removed by R8 full mode
- Type 23: DYNAMIC_PROXY_STRIPPED — Proxy.newProxyInstance() interface renamed
- Type 24: JSON_ASSET_RESOURCE_STRIPPED — resources referenced only in JSON assets
- Type 25: FRAGMENT_CLASS_RENAMED — FragmentManager saved state FQCN mismatch
- Type 26: SERIALIZABLE_CLASS_RENAMED — cross-build deserialization failure

Plugin improvements:
- Add assetsDirs property to PostBuildAnalysisTask for JSON asset scanning
- Fix SerializableAnalyzer to handle multi-line data class declarations
- Fix KOTLIN_OBJECT_INSTANCE_REMOVED: use renamed-field check not absence check
  (mapping.txt only lists renamed entries; absence means preserved, not removed)
- Fix DEVIRTUALIZATION_ILLEGAL_ACCESS: note whether class was renamed by keep rule
- Improve JAVASCRIPT_INTERFACE and NATIVE_METHOD fix rules to keep class itself
- Add fix rules for types 7, 14, 15, 18 (DataProcessor, WebBridgeHandler, etc.)
- Add HtmlReportGenerator exhaustive when branches for types 24-26

Verification results:
- Crash mode (no keep rules): 29 confirmed errors
- Fix mode (all keep rules applied): 1 remaining (devirtualization — inherent
  code issue, not preventable by ProGuard keep rules alone)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(spec): cover Deserialize error path when input is not a string

The `Deserialize for Spec` impl (spec.rs:293) calls
`String::deserialize(deserializer)?`.  Previously every call went through
the happy path (a JSON string) so the `?`-operator error branch — taken
when the JSON token is a number, bool, or object — was never exercised.

The new test `spec_deserialize_non_string_returns_error` passes the raw
JSON number `42` to `serde_json::from_str::<Spec>`, causing
`String::deserialize` to fail and the `?` error branch to be taken for
the first time.

Coverage delta (libaipm/src/spec.rs):
  - Regions missed:  17 → 16  (-1)
  - Lines missed:     4 →  4  (no change in line count, but region covered)
  - Branches missed: 13 → 13  (unchanged; LLVM counts `?` as region, not branch)
Overall TOTAL:
  - Missed regions:  2350 → 2348  (-2)
  - Missed lines:    1238 → 1237  (-1)
  - Branch coverage: 95.13%       (unchanged, already above 89% gate)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove step=1 from time inputs for minute-level picker (#172)

Remove step="1" attribute from all 4 time inputs (create form and edit
modal). Without it, browsers default to minute granularity, hiding the
seconds field from users. Seconds are still handled internally for
duration calculations.

Update e2e test to assert step attribute is absent rather than "1".

Bump version to 1.17.3.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(scaffolder): add TDD test suite for AzureSubscriptionPicker

11 tests across 5 describe blocks covering:
- Loading state: shows CircularProgress while ARM request is in flight
- Success path: dropdown renders, labels in 'Name (id)' format, onChange emits UUID
- Error fallback: text input shown when getAccessToken or ARM fetch fails
- Form restore: formData pre-selects the matching subscription on back-navigation
- Required/errors: Mui-required class applied, fallback field handles rawErrors

Testing notes:
- Mock microsoftAuthApiRef via TestApiProvider (not jest.mock on module level)
- MUI v4 combobox: role='combobox' is on the wrapper <div>, inner <input>
  accessed via querySelector('input') for value assertions
- Dropdown opened via ArrowDown keydown on inner input
- Option accessible names used for click targets (safe even with split DOM text)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: replace inline k* URL constants with AppConfig named endpoints

Replace all usages of kApiBaseUrl, kApi2BaseUrl, kApi3BaseUrl,
kApi9BaseUrl, kApi19BaseUrl, kRequestBaseUrl, kPaymentBaseUrl,
and kSocketServerBaseUrl across 63 Dart files with the corresponding
AppConfig.* named constants.

- Endpoint URLs → AppConfig.signIn, AppConfig.match, etc.
- Image URL prefixes → AppConfig.imageBase
- Local _baseUrl / baseUrl fields set to kApiBaseUrl/Api2 → AppConfig.imageBase
- Socket URL → AppConfig.socketUrl
- Payment URLs → AppConfig.khaltiPayment / AppConfig.hblPayment
- Query-param URLs keep their parameters, only the base+path is swapped

No new imports added; existing app_endpoints.dart re-export makes
AppConfig available wherever the old k* constants were used.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: marriagestationpvtltd-lang <254104945+marriagestationpvtltd-lang@users.noreply.github.com>
